profile.name: Michael Herrera
roles: Corporal, U.S. Marine Corps, 3rd Marine Expeditionary Brigade
profile.service_period: U.S. Marine Corps (served through at least 2009); 2023-present (public disclosure)
profile.summary: U.S. Marine Corps veteran who testified at the July 2023 House Oversight UAP hearing and filed a sworn ICIG complaint claiming his six-man patrol encountered a massive octagonal craft in Sumatra in 2009, being loaded by personnel in unmarked uniforms who subsequently threatened his patrol to silence. His account implies direct human interaction with non-human technology through a classified Special Access Program.
early_career: Enlisted in the U.S. Marine Corps; assigned to the 3rd Marine Expeditionary Brigade as a Corporal

key_events.event: Deployed to Sumatra, Indonesia on a humanitarian assistance mission following major earthquakes; claims his patrol encountered a massive octagonal craft approximately 300 feet in diameter being loaded by unidentified personnel in the jungle.
Herrera states his patrol was threatened by unknown personnel and ordered to remain silent; claims he was subsequently warned by military superiors not to discuss the incident.
Patrol escorted away from the site; Herrera alleges chain-of-command suppression of the incident began immediately following the encounter.

Filed a sworn statement with the Intelligence Community Inspector General under whistleblower protections; provided detailed account of the 2009 Sumatra incident after approximately 14 years of silence.
Testified at the House Oversight Subcommittee UAP hearing alongside David Grusch, Ryan Graves, and David Fravor; described the Sumatra encounter publicly for the first time under oath.
Gave multiple media interviews expanding on his account; stated that some members of his patrol have corroborated aspects of the incident in private but have not come forward publicly.

associated_people.name: David Grusch
associated_people.relationship: Both testified at the July 2023 House Oversight UAP hearing and filed whistleblower complaints with the ICIG; Grusch's broader SAP allegations provide institutional context for Herrera's account of human-operated UAP interaction.

associated_people.name: Ryan Graves
associated_people.relationship: Co-testified at the July 2023 House Oversight hearing; both are military veterans from different service branches who brought firsthand UAP accounts before Congress.

associated_people.name: Tim Burchett
associated_people.relationship: Co-organized the July 2023 House Oversight hearing and publicly supported Herrera's right to testify; one of the primary congressional architects of the 2023 UAP transparency push.

associated_people.name: Mike Gallagher
associated_people.relationship: Co-chaired the July 2023 hearing where Herrera testified; Gallagher's committee role directly enabled Herrera's public disclosure.

disclosures.title: ICIG Whistleblower Filing
disclosures.notes: Sworn statement filed with the Intelligence Community Inspector General under whistleblower protections detailing the 2009 Sumatra encounter and subsequent suppression by military superiors.

disclosures.title: House Oversight Subcommittee UAP Hearing
disclosures.outlet: U.S. House Oversight Subcommittee on National Security, the Border, and Foreign Affairs
disclosures.interviewer: Rep. Tim Burchett; Rep. Mike Gallagher
disclosures.notes: Testified under oath before the House Oversight Subcommittee; described the octagonal craft, the unknown personnel loading supplies, and the threats made against his patrol. Appeared alongside David Grusch, Ryan Graves, and David Fravor.

disclosures.title: Post-Hearing Media Interviews
disclosures.notes: Multiple television and podcast interviews following his congressional testimony, expanding on the Sumatra account and allegations of broader cover-up through classified Special Access Programs.

claims.claim: Herrera's patrol encountered a massive octagonal UAP approximately 300 feet in diameter in the Sumatran jungle in 2009.
claims.notes: Herrera has provided consistent accounts across testimony and interviews; he filed a sworn statement with the ICIG under penalty of perjury; his military service in Sumatra during this period is verifiable and consistent with documented humanitarian missions following the 2009 earthquakes. However, no other members of his patrol have publicly corroborated the account, and no physical evidence has been produced.

claims.claim: Unknown personnel in unmarked uniforms were actively loading the craft with supplies.
claims.notes: This is Herrera's stated claim from testimony and interviews. It implies a human-operated SAP with regular interaction with non-human technology. No corroborating witnesses have come forward publicly.

claims.claim: Herrera and his patrol were threatened to maintain silence and were subsequently subject to military suppression.
claims.notes: This pattern is consistent with accounts from other military UAP witnesses. Herrera filed under whistleblower protections, suggesting institutional seriousness regarding the threat of retaliation. No independent corroboration of the specific threats exists.

claims.claim: The UAP was being operated or maintained as part of a classified human-run Special Access Program.
claims.notes: This is the most extraordinary implication of Herrera's account. It is consistent with David Grusch's broader claims about UAP SAPs but cannot be independently verified. The specific claim of uniformed humans actively interfacing with a non-human craft distinguishes Herrera's account from all other 2023 hearing witnesses.

supporting: Active military service record is verifiable; deployment to Sumatra in 2009 is consistent with documented humanitarian missions following the September 2009 Sumatra earthquakes
Filed sworn statements under penalty of perjury with the ICIG, a formal legal mechanism
Account has been consistent across multiple interviews and sworn testimony
Testified before Congress publicly, accepting personal and professional exposure
Whistleblower protections indicate institutional seriousness regarding his disclosures
Account is internally coherent and operationally specific, including unit, location, date range, and patrol composition
The humanitarian mission context (post-earthquake Sumatra) is independently verifiable, placing his unit in that region at that time
contradicting: No members of his six-man patrol have publicly corroborated the account
No physical evidence has been produced
The account is the most operationally extraordinary of any 2023 hearing witness - direct human-UAP interaction goes beyond other contemporaneous testimony
The approximately 14-year delay before public disclosure introduces memory reliability concerns
He has not released the identities of patrol members who reportedly corroborated the account in private
The level of operational detail (octagonal shape, 300-foot diameter, supply loading) is highly specific in ways that are difficult to verify or falsify independently

career_connections.relationship: Both testified at the July 2023 House Oversight hearing; both filed whistleblower complaints with the ICIG regarding UAP programs
Co-testified at the July 2023 House Oversight hearing; both are military veterans and UAP witnesses from different service branches
Gallagher co-chaired the July 2023 hearing where Herrera testified
Burchett co-organized the July 2023 hearing and publicly supported Herrera's testimony
